volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 4.4.630c - Section 4.5.679b)

sections.label: Section 4.4.630c
sections.original: Vt mihi
sections.translation: According to what my mother told me , in the kitchen [*] , in a corner on the left hand .

sections.label: Section 4.4.632
sections.original: Haec erit tibi fausta meretrix : natast in calido loco ,
sections.translation: This woman will prove a lucky Courtesan for you ; she has been born in a warm spot , where full oft there is an abundance of all good things . ( Aside .) The Procurer was taken in when he asked where she was born .

sections.label: Section 4.4.635
sections.original: lepide lusit .
sections.translation: She has played him off nicely .

sections.label: Section 4.4.635b
sections.original: At ego patriam te rogo quae sit tua .
sections.translation: But I ask of you , what is your country ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.636
sections.original: Quae mihi sít , nisi haec ubi nunc sum ?
sections.translation: What should be mine but that where I now am ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.636b
sections.original: At ego illam quaero quae fuit .
sections.translation: But this I ’ m asking , what was ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.637
sections.original: Omne ego pro nihilo esse duco quod fuit , quando fuit :
sections.translation: Everything that was , do I consider as nothing , since it was , and is not now . Just like a man when he has breathed forth his spirit ; why enquire of him who he was ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.639
sections.original: Ita me di bene ament , sapienter . atque equidem miseret tamen .
sections.translation: So may the Deities kindly favour me , right cleverly . And yet I really do pity her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.640
sections.original: sed tamen , virgo , quae patriast tua , age mi actutum expedi .
sections.translation: But still , young woman , come , tell me at once which is your country ? Why are you silent ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.641b
sections.original: Dico equidem : quando hic servio , haec patriast mea .
sections.translation: For my part , I really am telling you my country . Since I ’ m in servitude here , this is my country .

sections.label: Section 4.4.642
sections.original: Iam de istoc rogare omitte ( non vides nolle eloqui ?)
sections.translation: Do cease now making enquiries about that . Don ’ t you see that she ’ s unwilling to declare , lest you should recall to her the remembrance of her misfortunes ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.643b
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? Is your father in captivity ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.644b
sections.original: Non captus , sed quod habuit perdidit .
sections.translation: Not in captivity ; but what he had , he has lost .

sections.label: Section 4.4.645
sections.original: Haec erit bono genere nata : nil scit nisi verum loqui .
sections.translation: She will prove to be born of a good family ; she knows how to say nothing but the truth .

sections.label: Section 4.4.646
sections.original: Quis fuit ? dic nomen .
sections.translation: Who was he ? Tell me his name ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.646b
sections.original: Quid illum miserum memorem qui fuit ?
sections.translation: Why should I tell of him , wretched man , who he was ? For the present ’ twere proper for him to be called Miserable , and me Miserable .

sections.label: Section 4.4.648
sections.original: Quoius modi is suo ín populo habitust ?
sections.translation: What kind of a person was he considered by the public ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.648b
sections.original: Nemo quisquam acceptior :
sections.translation: Not a person more acceptable ; slaves and free persons all liked him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.649b
sections.original: Hominem miserum praedicas ,
sections.translation: You do speak of a miserable man ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.650
sections.original: quom ét ipsus prorsus perditust et benevolentis perdidit .
sections.translation: inasmuch as he ’ s almost lost himself , and has lost his friends .

sections.label: Section 4.4.651
sections.original: Emam , opinor .
sections.translation: I shall purchase her , I think .

sections.label: Section 4.4.651b
sections.original: Etiam opinor ? summo genere esse arbitror ;
sections.translation: What , still I think ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.652
sections.original: divitias tu ex istac facies .
sections.translation: You ’ ll make riches by her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.652b
sections.original: Ita di faxint .
sections.translation: May the Gods grant it so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.652c
sections.original: Eme modo .
sections.translation: Do you only buy her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.653
sections.original: Iam hoc tibi dico : iam actutum ecastor meus pater , ubi me sciet
sections.translation: Now this I tell you : my father will be here directly , when he knows that I ’ ve been sold , and will ransom me thus separated from him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.654b
sections.original: Quid nunc ?
sections.translation: What say you now ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.654c
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.655
sections.original: Audin quid ait ?
sections.translation: Do you hear what she says ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.655b
sections.original: Nam etsi res sunt fractae , amici sunt tamen .
sections.translation: For although his fortunes are broken , he still has friends .

sections.label: Section 4.4.656
sections.original: Ne sis plora ; libera eris actutum , si crebro cades .
sections.translation: Don ’ t weep , please ; you ’ ll soon be at liberty , if — you have sweethearts enough [*] . Would you like to belong to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.657b
sections.original: Dum quidem ne nímis diu tua sim , volo .
sections.translation: So long , indeed , as I don ’ t belong to you too long . I ’ d like .

sections.label: Section 4.4.658
sections.original: Satin ut meminit libertatis ? dabit haec tibi grandis bolos .
sections.translation: How well she does keep in mind her liberty . She ’ ll be producing you fine hauls . About it , if you are about it . I ’ ll go back to him . ( To the YOUNG WOMAN .) Do you follow me . ( To SAGARISTIO ) I ’ ve brought her back to you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.660
sections.original: Adulescens , vin vendere istanc ?
sections.translation: Young man , are you disposed to sell her ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.660b
sections.original: Magis libet quam perdere .
sections.translation: I ’ d like it , rather than lose her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.661
sections.original: Tum tu pauca in verba confer : qui datur , tanti indica .
sections.translation: Do you compress it then into a few words ; state the price at which she ’ s offered .

sections.label: Section 4.4.662
sections.original: Faciam ita ut te velle video , út emas . habe centum minis .
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so , as I see you wish it . Take her for a hundred minae .

sections.label: Section 4.4.663
sections.original: Nimiumst .
sections.translation: That ’ s too much .

sections.label: Section 4.4.663b
sections.original: Octoginta .
sections.translation: For eighty .

sections.label: Section 4.4.663c
sections.original: Nimiumst .
sections.translation: That ’ s too much .

sections.label: Section 4.4.663d
sections.original: Nummus abesse hinc non potest ,
sections.translation: There can ’ t a didrachm be abated from the price which I shall now name .

sections.label: Section 4.4.664b
sections.original: Quid id est ergo ? eloquere actutum atque indica .
sections.translation: What is it , then ? Speak out at once and name it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.665
sections.original: Tuo periclo sexaginta haec dabitur argenti minis .
sections.translation: At your own risk , she ’ s offered at sixty minae .

sections.label: Section 4.4.666
sections.original: Toxilé , quid ago ?
sections.translation: Toxilus , what am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.666b
sections.original: Di deaeque te agitant irati , scelus ,
sections.translation: The Gods and Goddesses are pursuing you with their vengeance , you rogue , for not making haste to purchase her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.667b
sections.original: Habeto .
sections.translation: Take them , then .

sections.label: Section 4.4.667c
sections.original: Eu , praedatu'spraedatus es probe .
sections.translation: Well done , you have got a rich prize ! Be off , and fetch the money out here . On my faith , she ’ s not dear at three hundred minae .

sections.label: Section 4.4.669
sections.original: Heus tu , etiam pro vestimentis huc decem accedent minae .
sections.translation: Hark you , for her clothing there ’ ll be ten minae added to this as well .

sections.label: Section 4.4.670
sections.original: Abscedent enim , nón accedent .
sections.translation: Yes , be deducted , not added .

sections.label: Section 4.4.670b
sections.original: Tace sis , non tu illum vides
sections.translation: Do hold your tongue , will you ; don ’ t you see that he ’ s seeking an excuse to have the bargain broken ? Why don ’ t you be off and fetch the money ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.672
sections.original: Heus tu serva istum .
sections.translation: Hark you , do you keep an eye upon him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.672b
sections.original: Quin tu is intro ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you then go in ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.672c
sections.original: Abeo atque argentum affero .—
sections.translation: I ’ ll go and fetch the money .

sections.label: Section 4.5.673
sections.original: Edepol dedisti , virgo , operam adlaudabilem ,
sections.translation: Upon my word , young lady , you have given us praiseworthy aid , good . and wise , and sensible .

sections.label: Section 4.5.674b
sections.original: Si quid bonis
sections.translation: If for good persons

sections.label: Section 4.5.675
sections.original: boni fit , esse id et grave et gratum solet .
sections.translation: anything good is done , the same is wont to be both important and pleasing .

sections.label: Section 4.5.676
sections.original: Audin tu , Persa ? ubi argentum ab hoc acceperis ,
sections.translation: Do you hear , you Persian , when you ’ ve got the money of him , do you pretend as though you are going straight to the ship .

sections.label: Section 4.5.677b
sections.original: Ne doce .
sections.translation: Don ’ t teach me .

sections.label: Section 4.5.678
sections.original: Per angiportum rursum te ad me recipito
sections.translation: Betake yourself back again to our house , that way ( pointing .) down the lane through the garden .

sections.label: Section 4.5.679b
sections.original: Quod futurum est praedicas .
sections.translation: You are naming what ’ s intended to be done .
